behaviors.description: Static prompt-template lint rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local lint logic over template text (MOCK-MISS says this behavior is one-function/class validation). No real OS/network/subprocess is required (REAL-INFRA no). No model output dependence (LLM-DEP no). Rejected Integration+: behavior explicitly says "static prompt-template lint check"; that disqualifies higher levels.
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint function on template fixtures containing copied phase instructions from SKILL.md and assert it returns a rejection with precise location/reason; include a near-miss fixture to prevent overblocking.

behaviors.description: Fully assembled prompt from real harness interpolation/fixtures contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module handoff (template + interpolator + fixture loader + assembler), so Unit is too low for this behavior (MOCK-MISS yes). Integration brake: no real network/subprocess/OS-only phenomenon required; no AI-output correctness dependency; no multi-agent chain. Rejected System/Agentic by behavior text: "fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness" focuses on prompt construction, not live model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run real prompt assembly path with production-like fixtures and interpolation, then assert final prompt text has no leaked phase instructions or copied SKILL.md directives.

behaviors.description: Test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context, not hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: This behavior requires proving outcome is caused by skill context, i.e., model decision/content dependence (LLM-DEP yes). A mocked model could always pass and hide the real failure (MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejected Integration using behavior text "validates behavior from that context"; that is not just wiring, it is model-behavior effect.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a live model test where the same user-style prompt is used, skill context is loaded from actual plugin/skill files, and assertions verify behavioral output characteristics attributable to that context rather than explicit hard-coded prompt instructions.

behaviors.description: Harness can run the same user-style prompt against degraded/missing SKILL.md variant without changing prompt text.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is harness orchestration/config-file variant switching (module/file wiring). Integration brake: no real external infra requirement beyond local file loading; no need for model-output quality assertion in this behavior; no multi-step agent chain. Rejected Agentic using behavior text "can run ... without changing test prompt text" which is an execution-capability check.
behaviors.test_description: Run harness twice (baseline skill file and degraded/missing variant) with identical prompt text, asserting variant selection is applied through file/context loading only and invocation completes both times.

behaviors.description: If relevant SKILL.md section is removed/degraded, behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Pass/fail depends on live model behavior changing with skill-content regression (LLM-DEP yes). Stubbed outputs could mask the regression (MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejected Workflow: behavior describes one prompt/response comparison, not multiple real agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the same user-style prompt against baseline skill context and degraded context with live model execution; assert degraded case violates required behavior and test fails while baseline passes.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing/extraction logic.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering/instrumentation boundary across invocation, capture, and parser modules. No requirement for real model quality; a deterministic stub output can still expose sequencing bugs. Integration brake: no OS/network-only failure required by behavior text; no agentic correctness dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke harness with a fixed model-response fixture through the real capture+parse pipeline and assert diagnostic artifact is persisted with unmodified raw output before parser/extractor runs.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural constraints (schema paths, role-specific field placement), not just keywords.
behaviors.justification: Core failure boundary is assertion logic itself (schema/path/placement checks), reproducible with fixed fixtures in one module. No infra dependence; no real model dependence. Rejected Integration+: behavior is about assertion semantics, not harness/runtime interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Feed assertion engine crafted outputs: keyword-present but structurally invalid, and structurally valid controls; assert failures/successes follow structure rules rather than keyword hits.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions keyword-only checks miss.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires "live model execution" and regression detection depends on what the model actually returns (LLM-DEP yes). Deterministic mocks risk hiding this class of miss (MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejected Workflow: single live generation evaluated by stronger assertion, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run live prompt executions and evaluate outputs with both keyword-only and structural assertions; verify cases where keyword check passes but structural assertions fail, demonstrating real regression detection.
